heading: National Defense Sealift Fund
label: 2218 National Defense Sealift Fund
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsecs. (d)(3), (f)(2), and (k)(2)(A), is section 1424 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1991, which is set out as a note under .Section 1424 of Public Law 101–510section 7291 of this title

Codification, , , provided that section 1024 of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1993 [H.R. 5006, ], as it passed the Senate on , shall be amended in subsection 2218(c)(2) proposed for inclusion in this chapter by deleting all after “expended only” down to and including “appropriations Act” and inserting in lieu thereof “in amounts authorized by law”. It further provided that for purposes of that amendment, shall be treated as having been enacted after , regardless of the actual dates of enactment. The date of , referred to as the date the Senate passed the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1993, apparently is based on an order adopted by the Senate on [Cong. Rec., vol. 138, pt. 21, p. 30919] providing that when the conference report on the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1993 was received by the Senate from the House of Representatives it would be deemed to have been agreed to. On , the Senate received the conference report from the House, and it was considered adopted pursuant to that order [Cong. Rec., vol. 138, pt. 22, p. 31565].Pub. L. 102–396, title V106 Stat. 1896Pub. L. 102–484Pub. L. 102–396Pub. L. 102–484Oct. 6, 1992Oct. 3, 1992Oct. 3, 1992Oct. 3, 1992Oct. 5, 1992

C, title XXXV, § 3502132 Stat. 2308Aug. 13, 2018“The Secretary of Defense shall, consistent with , United States Code, use authority under section 2218 of such title to make such modifications to Ready Reserve Fleet vessels as are necessary for such vessels to comply [with] requirements for lifeboats and fire suppression under the International Convention for the Safety of Life at Sea by not later than .”section 2244a of title 10October 1, 2021

children.heading: .—Establishment
children.label: a .—Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury of the United States a fund to be known as the “National Defense Sealift Fund”.

children.heading: .—Administration of Fund
children.label: b .—Administration of Fund
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall administer the Fund consistent with the provisions of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: Construction (including design of vessels), purchase, alteration, and conversion of Department of Defense sealift vessels.

children.label: B
children.text: Operation, maintenance, and lease or charter of Department of Defense vessels for national defense purposes.

children.label: C
children.text: Installation and maintenance of defense features for national defense purposes on privately owned and operated vessels that are constructed in the United States and documented under the laws of the United States.

children.label: D
children.text: Expenses for maintaining the National Defense Reserve Fleet under and for the costs of acquisition of vessels for, and alteration and conversion of vessels in (or to be placed in), the fleet, but only for vessels built in United States shipyards.section 57100 of title 46
Funds in the National Defense Sealift Fund may be obligated or expended only in amounts authorized by law.
Funds obligated and expended for a purpose set forth in subparagraph (B) of paragraph (1) may be derived only from funds deposited in the National Defense Sealift Fund pursuant to subsection (d)(1).

children.label: A
children.text: construction (including design of vessels), purchase, alteration, and conversion of national defense sealift vessels;

children.label: B
children.text: operations, maintenance, and lease or charter of national defense sealift vessels; and

children.label: C
children.text: installation and maintenance of defense features for national defense purposes on privately owned and operated vessels.

All receipts from the disposition of national defense sealift vessels, excluding receipts from the sale, exchange, or scrapping of National Defense Reserve Fleet vessels under sections 57101–57104 and chapter 573 of title 46.
All receipts from the charter of vessels under section 1424(c) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1991 ( note).10 U.S.C. 8661
Any other funds made available to the Department of Defense to carry out any of the purposes described in subsection (c).

The Secretary of Defense may accept from any person, foreign government, or international organization any contribution of money, personal property (excluding vessels), or assistance in kind for support of the sealift functions of the Department of Defense.
Any contribution of property accepted under paragraph (1) may be retained and used by the Department of Defense or disposed of in accordance with procedures prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.
The Secretary of Defense shall deposit in the Fund money and receipts from the disposition of any property accepted under paragraph (1).
A vessel built in a foreign ship yard may not be purchased with funds in the National Defense Sealift Fund pursuant to subsection (c)(1), unless specifically authorized by law.

Construction, alteration, or conversion of vessels with funds in the National Defense Sealift Fund pursuant to subsection (c)(1) shall be conducted in United States ship yards and shall be subject to ().section 1424(b) of Public Law 101–510104 Stat. 1683

children.label: i
children.text: participated in the Maritime Security Fleet; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is available for purchase at a reasonable cost, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: B
children.text: If the Secretary determines that no used vessel meeting the requirements under clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (A) is available, the Secretary may purchase a used vessel comparable to a vessel described in clause (i) of subparagraph (A), regardless of the source of the vessel or where the vessel was constructed, if such vessel is available for purchase at a reasonable cost, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: C
children.text: The Secretary may not use the authority under this paragraph to purchase more than 12 foreign constructed vessels.

children.label: D
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the initial conversion, or modernization of any vessel purchased under the authority of subparagraph (A) occurs in a shipyard located in the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary has initiated an acquisition strategy for the construction in United States shipyards of not less than ten new vessels that are sealift vessels, auxiliary vessels, or a combination of such vessels; and

children.label: ii
children.text: of such new vessels, the lead ship is anticipated to be delivered by not later than 2028.

children.label: i
children.text: The proposed date of the purchase.

children.label: ii
children.text: The price at which the vessel would be purchased.

children.label: iii
children.text: The anticipated cost of modernization of the vessel.

children.label: iv
children.text: The proposed military utility of the vessel.

children.label: v
children.text: The proposed date on which the vessel will be available for use by the Ready Reserve.

children.label: vi
children.text: The contracting office responsible for the completion of the purchase.

children.label: I
children.text: there was no vessel available for purchase at a reasonable price that was constructed in the United States; and

children.label: II
children.text: the used vessel purchased supports the recapitalization of the Ready Reserve Force component of the National Defense Reserve Fleet or the Military Sealift Command surge fleet.

children.label: viii
children.text: A detailed account of the criteria used to make the determination under subparagraph (B).

children.label: G
children.text: The Secretary may not finalize or execute the final purchase of any vessel using the authority under this paragraph until 30 days after the date on which a report under subparagraph (F) is submitted with respect to such purchase.

children.heading: 5 .—Expiration of Funds AfterYears
children.label: g 5 .—Expiration of Funds AfterYears
children.text: No part of an appropriation that is deposited in the National Defense Sealift Fund pursuant to subsection (d)(1) shall remain available for obligation more than five years after the end of fiscal year for which appropriated except to the extent specifically provided by law.
the amount requested for programs, projects, and activities for construction (including design of vessels), purchase, alteration, and conversion of national defense sealift vessels;
the amount requested for programs, projects, and activities for operation, maintenance, and lease or charter of national defense sealift vessels;

the amount requested for programs, projects, and activities for installation and maintenance of defense features for national defense purposes on privately owned and operated vessels that are constructed in the United States and documented under the laws of the United States; and
the amount requested for programs, projects, and activities for research and development relating to national defense sealift.

children.heading: .—Title or Management of Vessels
children.label: i .—Title or Management of Vessels
children.text: Nothing in this section (other than subsection (c)(1)(D)) shall be construed to affect or modify title to, management of, or funding responsibilities for, any vessel of the National Defense Reserve Fleet, or assigned to the Ready Reserve Force component of the National Defense Reserve Fleet, as established by .section 57100 of title 46

children.heading: .—Contracts for Incorporation of Defense Features in Commercial Vessels
children.label: j .—Contracts for Incorporation of Defense Features in Commercial Vessels

children.text: The head of an agency may enter into a contract with a company submitting an offer for that company to install and maintain defense features for national defense purposes in one or more commercial vessels owned or controlled by that company in accordance with the purpose for which funds in the National Defense Sealift Fund are available under subsection (c)(1)(C). The head of the agency may enter into such a contract only after the head of the agency makes a determination of the economic soundness of the offer. As consideration for a contract with the head of an agency under this subsection, the company entering into the contract shall agree with the Secretary of Defense to make any vessel covered by the contract available to the Secretary, fully crewed and ready for sea, at any time at any port determined by the Secretary, and for whatever duration the Secretary determines necessary.

children.label: A
children.text: The costs to build, procure, and install a defense feature in the vessel.

children.label: B
children.text: The costs to periodically maintain and test any defense feature on the vessel.

children.label: C
children.text: Any increased costs of operation or any loss of revenue attributable to the installation or maintenance of any defense feature on the vessel.

children.label: D
children.text: Any additional costs associated with the terms and conditions of the contract.

children.label: E
children.text: Payments of such sums as the Government would otherwise expend, if the vessel were placed in the Ready Reserve Fleet, for maintaining the vessel in the status designated as “ROS–4 status” in the Ready Reserve Fleet for 25 years.

For any contract under paragraph (1) under which the United States makes advance payments under paragraph (2) for the costs associated with installation or maintenance of any defense feature on a commercial vessel, the contractor shall provide to the United States such security interests in the vessel, by way of a preferred mortgage under or otherwise, as the head of the agency may prescribe in order to adequately protect the United States against loss for the total amount of those costs.section 31322 of title 46

children.label: A
children.text: set forth terms and conditions under which, so long as a vessel covered by the contract is owned or controlled by the contractor, the contractor is to operate the vessel for the Department of Defense notwithstanding any other contract or commitment of that contractor; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide that the contractor operating the vessel for the Department of Defense shall be paid for that operation at fair and reasonable rates.
The head of an agency may not delegate authority under this subsection to any officer or employee in a position below the level of head of a procuring activity.

The head of an agency may not enter into a contract under paragraph (1) that would provide for payments to the contractor as authorized in paragraph (2)(E) until notice of the proposed contract is submitted to the congressional defense committees and a period of 90 days has elapsed.
The term “Fund” means the National Defense Sealift Fund established by subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: A fast sealift ship, including any vessel in the Fast Sealift Program established under ().section 1424 of Public Law 101–510104 Stat. 1683

children.label: B
children.text: Any other auxiliary vessel that was procured or chartered with specific authorization in law for the vessel, or class of vessels, to be funded in the National Defense Sealift Fund.

children.label: A
children.text: a Department of Defense sealift vessel; and

children.label: B
children.text: a national defense reserve fleet vessel, including a vessel in the Ready Reserve Force maintained under .section 57100 of title 46
The term “head of an agency” has the meaning given that term in .section 3004 of this title
The term “Maritime Security Fleet” means the fleet established under .section 53102(a) of title 46
